game_description: Deduce the murderer, weapon, and room through elimination, 3-6 players.

strategies.strategy_detailed: Mark your own cards first — those are eliminated from suspicion. Then use a column per player on your sheet. When a player shows you a card, mark it. When a player shows nothing on your suggestion, mark them as lacking all three of those cards. Missing marks are as valuable as direct reveals.
strategy_stealth: Mark own cards first — they're cleared
Column per player on your sheet
No-show = lacks all 3 of your cards

strategies.strategy_detailed: Move toward rooms adjacent to your starting position you don't hold. Use the diagonal secret passages (Lounge↔Conservatory, Kitchen↔Study) to swap corners in one turn — free travel that ignores dice. Players stranded in hallways while others gather information fall badly behind.
strategy_stealth: Adjacent rooms first; suggest each round

strategies.strategy_detailed: Suggest the suspect you know least about first. You already know your own cards — don't waste suggestions confirming suspects you hold. Work through the unknowns systematically: 6 suspects × 6 weapons × 9 rooms — eliminate suspects first, then weapons, then rooms.
strategy_stealth: Suggest unknowns — not your own cards

strategies.condition: Showing a card to another player
strategies.strategy_detailed: If you've already shown a card to the asker before, repeat it — zero info leak. Otherwise, show from the category where the asker has the MOST remaining unknowns (suspects, weapons, or rooms). Avoid showing from categories near certainty — those leak the most info per card revealed.
strategy_stealth: Repeat cards already shown to asker

strategies.strategy_detailed: A no-show means the passing player holds none of the three cards you suggested. Mark all three as 'not held' by that player. If 2 players pass on the same suggestion, you've eliminated 6 cards from two players' hands — often enough to deduce ownership of specific cards.

When two suspects remain unknown, suggest one specifically with a weapon and room you know are in players' hands. If someone shows you the weapon or room, you know the suspect was the mystery card — you've deduced the murderer without them showing the suspect directly.
strategy_stealth: Showing weapon/room = suspect is the murderer

strategies.strategy_detailed: If an opponent is making very specific suggestions (only one unknown in each set), they're close to the solution. Move into rooms they haven't visited to force them to suggest you there — their suggestion will reveal what they're testing. Use the information to update your own sheet.
strategy_stealth: Their suggestion to you reveals target card

strategies.condition: Deducing a card no one has shown
strategies.strategy_detailed: If multiple DIFFERENT players have suggested a card and no one ever shows it, the card must be in the envelope (it can't be in multiple hands at once). 3-4 distinct suggesters = highly conclusive. One player repeating doesn't qualify — they might hold it. Confirm by suggesting with 2 cards you hold.

strategies.condition: Ready to make a final accusation
strategies.strategy_detailed: Accusations don't require being in a room — only suggestions do. So once certain, accuse on your turn from wherever you stand. Don't telegraph readiness; opponents will race to accuse first. If 2 elements are certain and 1 is 50/50, guess — being pre-empted is worse than a coin-flip wrong call.
strategy_stealth: Accuse from anywhere — not a room

strategies.strategy_detailed: You cannot stop a correct accusation. Accuse first if you have a reasonable guess. A wrong accusation eliminates you but keeps the game alive — sometimes a wrong guess by the leader saves everyone else from losing to their correct one.
strategy_stealth: You can't block a correct accusation
Accuse first if you have reasonable guess
Wrong accusation = you're out but game continues
